title: PairwiseMatch v1
description: Record of one cross-family pairwise comparison between two idea nodes under pre-committed review criteria. Each panel seat votes with anchored arguments only (literature or computation anchors); unanchored arguments are discarded and counted. The outcome is written back to the external belief graph as an observation.

criteria_commitment.description: Review criteria committed before the panel sees the pair, so the criteria cannot be adjusted to fit a preferred outcome.

panel.description: One entry per judge vote. A valid match holds at least three votes from DISTINCT model families. JSON Schema cannot express "families must be distinct", so minItems: 3 here is only the closest schema-expressible floor; the authoritative check on family diversity and the three-distinct-families rule is the Python validator validate_pairwise_match in skills/idea-pairwise-match/scripts/assemble_match.py.

unanchored_arguments_discarded.description: Count of arguments this seat produced that carried no literature/computation anchor and were therefore discarded before voting.

observation_write.description: Whether the match outcome has been written to the external belief graph as an observation.

gaia_package_ref.description: Optional reference to the belief-graph package that received the observation.

independent_runners.description: Whether the panel seats resolved to genuinely distinct underlying runner commands. False marks a stub-backed or single-command panel whose votes carry no real cross-family diversity, so the belief layer can weight the observation accordingly. Read from the panel run report at assembly time.
